graphEvents.label: P1 live respawn lock can be reclaimed by age

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Cross-phase correctness revisit spans embedding startup/reindex, daemon shutdown/WAL, and launcher/model-server single-writer lease surfaces.

targetSelection.selectionReason: Selected cross-phase integration seams called out by iteration state: lazy model-server lock ownership, launcher shutdown, daemon WAL close ordering, active embedder startup, reindex worker lifecycle, and standalone save locks.

findingDetails.title: Lazy model-server respawn locks expire while the live listener still owns them
findingDetails.claim: The demand listener holds the respawn lock for the whole listener lifetime, but stale detection reclaims locks older than 60 seconds even when the owner PID is alive.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked release sites and cross-launcher tests; immediate contention is covered, but no test covers a live-owner lock older than RESPAWN_LOCK_STALE_MS.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: The age threshold is valid for orphan cleanup of short critical sections, but it conflicts with the documented long-lived lazy listener lock.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade only if another guard prevents a second launcher from reaching acquireDemandRespawnLock after 60 seconds while the first listener remains alive.

searchLedger.hypothesis: A lock intentionally held for the lazy listener lifetime can be age-reclaimed while the owner process is alive.

searchActions.result: RESPAWN_LOCK_STALE_MS is 60000; isRespawnLockStale reclaims age-old locks even after confirming only that dead PIDs are stale; comments say the lock is intentionally held across the listener window.
Immediate second launcher blocking is covered; live-owner aged-lock blocking is not.

searchLedger.rationale: The stale policy contradicts the long-lived lock ownership contract and can reopen duplicate listener races.

searchLedger.invariant: Shutdown should drain watcher work before checkpointing and closing the vector DB/WAL.

searchActions.result: close_db checkpoints attached vector and main WAL before close and removes the unclean marker only on main checkpoint success.

searchLedger.rationale: No new shutdown/WAL ordering finding in this pass.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Drain-before-close and checkpoint-before-close ordering are present in the reviewed code.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Prior correctness findings may be rediscovered in the embed startup/reindex seam.

searchActions.result: Observed the same prior provider-order and cancellation lifecycle risks; no new extension beyond existing registry entries.

searchLedger.rationale: Already captured by DR-001-P1-001 and DR-001-P1-002; this iteration reports new findings only.
searchLedger.notApplicableReason: Prior findings were confirmed but not extended or escalated.

searchLedger.hypothesis: The standalone save lock and daemon lease check may contain a new cross-phase correctness issue beyond the prior workflow fail-open finding.

searchActions.result: Observed the prior fail-open timeout path and shared daemon liveness probe; no distinct new integration issue found.

searchLedger.rationale: No new standalone-save finding beyond DR-002-P1-002.
searchLedger.notApplicableReason: Existing registry already covers the actionable fail-open behavior.

title: Lazy model-server respawn locks expire while the live listener still owns them
claim: The demand listener holds the respawn lock for the whole listener lifetime, but stale detection reclaims locks older than 60 seconds even when the owner PID is alive.

detail: New correctness P1; not a duplicate of prior workflow-lock fail-open because this is the model-server respawn lock used by lazy demand listeners.
Finding tied to committed model-server supervision code and phase 002/005 cross-launcher lock contract.
Immediate lock contention is tested, but aged live-owner lock contention is not.

reason: context-server drains fileWatcher before vectorIndex.closeDb and close_db checkpoints attached/main WAL before close.
Provider override ordering and reindex cancellation remain prior findings without new escalation evidence.

event.label: P1 live respawn lock can be reclaimed by age
